_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MS/ DNB/PG Diploma or equivalent (in ENT, General Surgery, Pediatric Surgery)
clinical_indications: Presence of foreign body (FB) in ear, nose, or throat, particularly in children.
FB causing mild disturbances up to life-threatening complications.
FB in airway or oesophagus presenting with life-threatening breathing difficulty, history of aspiration/ingestion, unexplained chest infection, or dysphagia.
Diagnosis confirmed by clinical signs, symptoms, and examination.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the day care procedure.
X-ray report/ clinical picture of the affected part confirming the diagnosis and justify the procedure.

claim.label: Post procedure clinical photograph/ relevant imaging study for pre and post procedure comparison

ppd_preauth.q: Were the Clinical notes including evaluation findings, indication for procedure, and planned line of management, advise for the day care procedure submitted?
Was the X-ray/ clinical picture of the affected part confirming the diagnosis and justify the procedure submitted?
Are the Clinical notes and X-ray/clinical picture of affected part indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed Indoor case papers submitted?
Was Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the post procedure clinical photograph/ relevant imaging study for pre and post procedure comparison submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or X-ray/clinical picture of affected part are not indicative of the procedure.
Pre-authorization clinical notes (including evaluation findings, indication, management plan) are missing or incomplete.
X-ray report or clinical picture of the affected part is missing or does not confirm the diagnosis/justify the procedure at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor case papers are missing at claim submission.
Procedure note or operative note is missing at claim submission.
Post-procedure clinical photograph or relevant imaging study for pre and post procedure comparison is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.
Ear, nose and throat (ENT) foreign bodies (FBs) are common occurrences, particularly among children.
Consequences of FBs are variable, from mild disturbances to life-threatening complications.
Variability is related to chemical composition, shape, dimensions of FBs, and anatomical site involved (ear, nose, oral cavity (fish bones etc) as well as inhaled and ingested FB).

FBs in airway and oesophagus may present in emergency with life-threatening breathing difficulty, with a history of foreign body aspiration/ ingestion and as unexplained chest infection/ dysphagia based on the site of lodgment and nature of the foreign body material.
surgical_options: Removal of FB from nasal cavity, ear, and oral cavity following visualization of the FB and under adequate light using appropriate instruments.
Sedation or general anesthesia may be required for pediatric, apprehensive, or deeply impacted painful FBs.
Endoscopes and microscopes may be used for FB removal from nose/throat and ear.
Direct laryngoscopy and rigid trachea-bronchoscopy method for FBs in the larynx and airway.
Flexible or rigid oesophagoscopy may be used for oesophageal foreign bodies.
referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care as required based on the patient's condition and complexity.
